phase-plan.purpose: Build the first complete in-house CRM layer for Grime Time by mirroring the useful structure of HubSpot inside Payload and Next.js: linked business records, search and queue views, compact operator UI, Stripe billing sync, and Payload-jobs-driven Resend sequences.
phase-plan.scope: In scope: first-party CRM schema, compact `/ops` CRM workspace, owner assignment, pipeline and stale-follow-up views, multi-role employee duties, strict customer/admin data boundaries, fastest reminder delivery to workers' normal inboxes, Stripe webhook reconciliation into billing records, Payload jobs and Resend sequences, demo data, and test coverage. Out of scope: reintroducing HubSpot or EngageBay, rebuilding every HubSpot setting screen, or exposing admin-only CRM controls in the customer portal.

file.description: Capability map from HubSpot primitives into first-party Payload CRM, Stripe sync, and sequence automation.
Product direction and design rules for the in-house CRM, billing, and lifecycle automation.
Billing operating model for Stripe-hosted invoices, portal-first self-service, subscriptions, refunds/credits, and onsite/out-of-band payment handling.
System-of-record boundaries for Payload, Stripe, Resend, Supabase, and jobs.
Daily operating model for role duties, queue triage rhythm, SLA expectations, and reminder delivery policy.
Frontend and code-quality standards for icon usage, file-size guardrails, literal centralization, and reusable extraction patterns.
Target folder structure and phased extraction roadmap for reusable ops/auth/crm/dashboard architecture.
Payload collections, globals, jobs, email adapter, and MCP/plugin boundaries.
Current CRM boundary where third-party sync is disabled and first-party CRM should take over.
Shared CRM option sets for status, priority, sources, tasks, and automation enrollment state.
Admin CRM workspace and compact operating console target.
Compact non-overlapping command-center shell for ops sections, including the CRM workspace tab.
First-party CRM tab surface using queue groups, metric chips, and inline detail loading.
App-wide provider composition where TanStack Query should live for mutable portal and ops surfaces.
Shared query-client and invalidation helpers for server-first plus TanStack Query patterns.
Admin-only queue/summary feed for the compact CRM workspace.
Admin-only record detail feed for CRM list/detail interactions.
Admin-only CRM activity mutation route for inline note logging from the workspace.
Shared server-side query and detail helpers backing `/ops` and internal CRM APIs.
CRM activity-note creation helpers that map selected records into first-party timeline entries.
Portal navigation rules separating admin CRM tools from customer account tasks.
Portal shell composition (sidebar/inset/toolbar layering) that must avoid overlap and preserve conventional app chrome behavior.
Structured CRM detail rail with summary/related tabs and compact record context.
Inline internal note composer for the CRM detail rail.
Existing quote records that should feed internal opportunities and follow-up state.
First-party account records for households and commercial relationships.
First-party contact records for people, consent state, and preferred communication method.
Inbound lead records before conversion.
Quote and pipeline opportunity records.
Activity timeline records for notes, calls, and system events.
Sequence-definition builder records for in-app follow-up automation design.
Task records for follow-up, billing, and schedule work.
Sequence enrollment records for Payload jobs and Resend automation.
Internal invoice records that must reconcile with Stripe webhook events.
Internal billing event ledger for Stripe webhooks, manual payment entries, credits, and refunds.
Recurring-plan records that will carry subscription state and billing cadence.
Service schedule records that connect CRM ownership, customer portal scheduling, and ops calendar views.
Environment contract for Stripe, Resend, cron, and optional Payload MCP setup.
Stripe webhook ingestion endpoint for invoice, payment, refund, and subscription reconciliation.
Customer-facing route for creating Stripe billing portal sessions from the account/invoice views.
Cron-triggered jobs endpoint that runs Payload schedules and queued lifecycle work.
Payload jobs registration for billing scans, customer notifications, and sequence processing.
Shared customer lifecycle email builders for lead, quote, invoice, reminder, and appointment delivery.
Shared claim/invite CTA provisioning used by lifecycle emails and activation flows.

task.description: Create the first-party CRM collections, relationships, stages, assignment rules, commercial-account fields, and shared field vocabulary needed for leads through retained customers. This is now complete for the current scope: the repo has first-party CRM collections, company-account membership, account-linked customer documents, centralized task-policy builders, default owner routing fallback, and typed task metadata for source, SLA, role tags, next action, and escalation timing.

Build internal CRM API routes and query helpers for queue views, stale follow-up, task management, notes, activities, detail summaries, owner assignment, and lead qualification/disqualification. Queue/detail GET routes, search, task updates, opportunity stage actions, note creation, owner reassignment, lead qualification/disqualification, and owner/commercial filtering now exist; saved views and deeper outreach logging remain future enhancement work.

Turn `/ops` into the compact CRM workspace with queue tables, tabs, tab groups, button groups, icons, badges, info icons, tooltips, priorities, and a non-overlapping list/detail layout that avoids overflow-heavy interactions. The compact workspace is now live with search, stale-only filtering, owner and commercial filters, quick actions, a structured detail rail, inline note logging, owner reassignment, richer company detail, and an impersonation-aware customer preview toolbar that stays visible across frontend and portal routes.

Implement Stripe webhook ingestion and idempotent reconciliation so internal invoice and service-plan state is trustworthy. This slice is now live with Stripe customer/invoice/subscription mapping, webhook reconciliation, monthly consolidated invoice helpers for commercial accounts, hosted invoice and billing-portal flows, compact in-app admin billing controls for payment/discount/credit/refund/write-off actions, and a first-party billing-event ledger instead of relying on raw collection editing for daily work.

Implement Payload-jobs-driven Resend sequences with the fastest practical path first: reminders and digests to each worker's preferred existing inbox, then optional mailbox-provider OAuth expansion after core queue reliability. The current repo now includes Payload job tasks, a cron-driven job runner, seeded sequence definitions, customer lifecycle email builders, overdue invoice scans, and hook-driven enrollment/notification triggers for leads, quotes, invoices, and service appointments.

Populate demo data and cover the CRM, billing, and customer account flows with unit, integration, and Playwright tests. This is now complete for the current scope: demo seed data includes commercial billing defaults and user overrides, unit coverage exists for operating-rhythm and discount-policy logic, integration coverage exercises CRM and billing workspace behavior, and Playwright admin/frontend portal coverage passes against the production-style app server.

Define the role matrix and operating rhythm so staff know expected daily duties, queue order, SLA response windows, escalation ownership, and support/refund policy queue behavior. This is now complete for the current scope: multi-role duties, default owner routing, SLA classes, escalation timestamps, required next-action fields, and support/refund routing are all encoded through shared operating-rhythm and CRM task-policy modules and surfaced in `/ops` duty summaries.

Codify and enforce boundary policy: public users and logged-in customers only see public + self/account-scoped data; customer-company admins can manage users within their own company only; staff/admin views can access cross-account data and internal docs. The current repo enforces this across effective-user auth, company account membership, portal nav, impersonation guards, and customer-facing route/data constraints.

Keep local MCP usage repeatable for Codex/Cursor by documenting and validating endpoint/key/transport health checks for payload_local and image_gen_local. This is now backed by `npm run verify:mcp`, which checks editor config stubs, Payload MCP endpoint reachability/auth acceptance, and image-gen stdio startup from repo scripts.

Use conventional shadcn app-shell patterns for sidebar and content insets so the portal avoids overlay collisions, horizontal overflow, and hidden admin toolbar controls. This is now complete for the current scope: shell variables are centralized, the floating admin preview toolbar uses the shared shell offset, sticky rails respect a shared portal top offset, the preview toolbar can collapse into a draggable corner chip, and top-corner preview placement no longer sits on top of the workspace header.

Keep `/ops` as the KPI-first staff dashboard and move dense daily work into `/ops/workspace`, where today board, ops workspace queues, billing follow-up, scorecard, milestones, and assets share one non-overlapping tabbed surface. Legacy `/ops/*` section routes remain as compatibility redirects into the workspace so deep links still resolve without leaving the consolidated operating surface.

Enforce deterministic customer preview in staff portal: `Customer home` binds to a stable non-admin `test_user` context, sidebar labels show active context (`admin` vs preview user), and admin-only nav/docs/settings hide while previewing customer scope.

Apply and enforce coding conventions for shared UI/system quality: icon usage standards, reduced copy-heavy/faded UI patterns, large-file extraction, constants/typed unions for repeated literals, reusable utilities for recurring patterns, auth/account surfaces that use small focused modules instead of monolithic form files, and server-first plus TanStack Query patterns for live mutable client workflows. This is now complete for the current scope: repeated role/source/SLA literals are centralized, policy objects drive CRM and billing behavior, large billing UI was split into smaller modules, and live mutable ops/customer surfaces follow the shared server-first plus TanStack Query pattern.

Execute phased extraction toward the target folder structure, including ops page loader/presenter splits, nav/auth context presenters, shared status/UI metadata, typed query-param utilities for `/ops/*` route split, and smaller auth/portal-access feature modules for claim/invite/account-entry flows. This is now complete for the current scope: focused `/ops/*` routing, shared query helpers, portal-shell extraction, policy/data builders for CRM tasks, and smaller billing workspace components are all in place without stop-the-world rewrites.

questions.question: Inbound public submissions create a `lead` first; automatic `account`/`contact` creation for every visitor is out of scope. Promotion to account/contact is explicit when qualified (see DECISIONS `D-crm-002`).

Default owner routing is now deterministic: use explicit preferred owner first, then the account owner, then the stable seeded admin roster from `resolveSeedStaffEmails()`, then the first admin user in created order (`src/lib/crm/internal/ownerRouting.ts`).

Customer-facing lead, quote, invoice, and post-service follow-up emails may auto-send from active sequence definitions, while create-task sequence steps are the default path for internal handoffs, no-reply follow-up, and overdue billing escalation (`src/endpoints/seed/crm-sequences.ts`, `src/lib/automation/sequences/process.ts`).

Commercial workflows now surface billing/AP and policy context by default through account fields and workspace detail: AP email/phone, billing terms, tax-exempt state, location count, portal access mode, billing rollup mode, and account default discount policy (`src/collections/Accounts/index.ts`, `src/lib/crm/workspace/detail.ts`, `src/lib/billing/workspace.ts`).

Owner reassignment and lead qualification/disqualification are now inline in the CRM workspace. The next likely inline CRM action beyond the current scope is customer outreach logging and saved-view support.

Stable recurring customers can use Stripe subscription/autopay flows, while commercial terms accounts and monthly rollup customers default to send-invoice behavior with monthly consolidated invoicing and explicit terms tracking (`.planning/workflows/stripe-billing-and-customer-payment-operations.md`, `src/collections/Accounts/index.ts`).

Current first-class billing actions are paid onsite, paid by check/out-of-band, discount, courtesy credit, refund, and write-off; these remain staff-only controls backed by the billing event ledger and compact ops billing UI.
Use server components for first load and TanStack Query in client islands for live mutable portal and ops data (CRM, billing, invites, account mutations) instead of ad hoc fetch-state islands.

Day-one impersonation scope is the marketing site plus the customer portal routes for dashboard, estimates, invoices, schedule, and account management. Internal staff views such as `/ops` and admin docs redirect out when the effective user is non-admin.
Fastest rollout: reminders go to each worker's preferred existing email address (personal or company) via Resend delivery, without requiring Google/Microsoft mailbox OAuth for day one.
Employees may hold multiple internal roles simultaneously; queue permissions and duties are additive across assigned roles.

Anything that exposes cross-user or cross-company data, internal docs, or operational controls is admin/staff-only. Customer and public surfaces remain constrained to public + self/account-scoped data.

Support SLA defaults are now encoded in the shared task-policy layer: general support and billing support acknowledge within one business day, refund requests acknowledge within one business day with same-day escalation, and policy/privacy requests route to ops-admin immediately with one-business-day acknowledgment (`src/lib/crm/tasks/policy.ts`, `src/lib/ops/policies/operatingRhythm.ts`).

Refund requests route to both `billing-followup` and `ops-admin`: billing-followup owns initial review/documentation, while ops-admin remains part of the task ownership/escalation path for approval and final disposition (`src/lib/crm/tasks/policy.ts`).

Discount hierarchy is now explicit and implemented: user-level override wins first, then account-level default, then base pricing; both percent and flat amount variants are supported (`src/lib/billing/discountPolicy.ts`, `src/collections/Accounts/index.ts`, `src/collections/Users/index.ts`).
Company-admin customers can manage users and company-admin membership within their company and can view company billing context including invoices, subscriptions, and payment methods.
CRM workspace and other dense ops sections should be split into dedicated `/ops/*` routes instead of being crowded inside one command-center surface.
Internal command center should focus on currently logged-in worker duties and link out to specialized ops pages via URLs and query params.
Admin impersonation toolbar should remain top-layer and visible; sidebar/content layout must not obscure it.
Staff `Customer home` should default to a stable non-admin `test_user` preview identity for repeatable customer-flow checks.
Seed contract should retain three admin staff users (`bg@`, `pb@`, `de@`) and one non-admin preview user (`test_user`) for deterministic testing.
Adopt soft file-size guardrails and extraction triggers to prevent growth of monolithic components/routes.
Repeated status/stage/role and UI label literals should move to shared constants/typed unions instead of ad hoc strings spread across files.
Adopt measurable extraction thresholds (repeated literals/UI/mappers, file/function size guardrails) so DRY/SOLID enforcement is objective during implementation reviews.
Adopt a shared pattern catalog (config-driven UI, policy objects, feature modules, list-detail shells, typed query-param state) for new ops/customer work.
Use the refactor extraction roadmap as the target folder/module structure and migrate incrementally during normal feature delivery.
